# Tabletop Encounter Budgeting

This note belongs to a different discipline entirely: budgeting monster
encounters per session in a tabletop role-playing campaign, tracking
experience-point totals against a party's level to keep a combat
encounter from being a rout in either direction.

## Encounter difficulty tables

A game master cross-references party level against a monster's
challenge rating in a lookup table before placing it in a dungeon room,
adjusting for the number of players seated at the table that session.

## Session logistics

None of this concerns branching narrative structure, prose voice, or
gamebook pacing — it is workshop material for a different kind of game
entirely, kept here only to prove that folder scoping excludes it.
